dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Clean dependency direction with no cycles. The __main__.py properly imports from regicide_image_builder. Module boundaries align with domain concepts. Minor deduction for lack of explicit __all__ exports in some modules.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: Mixed naming conventions in test directories: unit/ has 25 camelCase identifiers vs 169 snake_case. This indicates naming convention drift in test methods that should be standardized.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: Mixed error strategies (throws in production, try-catch in tests) which is appropriate for different layers. Production code uses exceptions properly. Minor inconsistency in error message formatting across modules.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: No unnecessary wrappers. Direct domain APIs. Good use of dataclasses for configuration. Minor deduction for some functions with 7+ parameters in CLI handlers.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: No circular dependencies detected. External dependencies are minimal and appropriate (typer, rich, pydantic). Good separation of concerns.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Comprehensive test coverage for production code. Tests use pytest with asyncio markers appropriately. Some test files are large (>300 LOC) but this is structural, not strategic.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: build-system/tests/__init__.py has 100% comment ratio with minimal content, suggesting AI-generated boilerplate that wasn't cleaned up.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Clear separation between build-system and tests. Package structure follows Python conventions. Minor improvement would be adding a src/ layout.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Good decomposition with clear domain alignment. BuildConfig, BuildResult, and RegicideImageBuilder classes represent core concepts well. Async patterns are appropriate for I/O-bound operations.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: Good handoffs between functions. Explicit boundaries between build phases. Minor improvement possible in reducing optional parameter count.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Direct control flow with minimal defensive sprawl. Type annotations are consistent. Good use of context managers for resource handling. Some magic numbers remain despite constants being added.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Functions are focused with single responsibilities. Parameter lists are reasonable for CLI handlers. Return types are explicit and consistent. Minor improvement in reducing cyclomatic complexity in build_image method.

findings.summary: Mixed naming conventions in test directories: unit/ has 25 camelCase identifiers vs 169 snake_case
findings.evidence: Blind packet shows camelCase count of 25 in unit/ vs snake_case count of 169, indicating naming convention drift in test methods
findings.suggestion: Standardize test method names to snake_case following Python conventions. For example, 'testCreateImage' → 'test_create_image'.

findings.summary: build-system/tests/__init__.py has 100% comment ratio with minimal content
findings.evidence: Blind packet shows 100% comment ratio for build-system/tests/__init__.py, indicating the file contains only a comment
findings.suggestion: Either remove the comment or add meaningful module documentation. An empty file is acceptable for namespace packages.

strengths: Clean module decomposition with clear domain boundaries
Consistent use of dataclasses for configuration objects
Good async/await patterns in build pipeline
